title: The Hokkaido Birth Cohort Study on Environment and Children's Health: cohort profile-updated 2017.
ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective birth cohort study using direct specimen collection (blood, hair, breast milk) and face-to-face tests, not EHR or linked health data.
summary: An update on the Hokkaido Study on Environment and Children’s Health, a prospective birth cohort study in Japan investigating the effects of environmental chemical exposures on birth outcomes, child development, and genetic/epigenetic markers.
key_findings: Environmental chemical exposures demonstrate effects on genetically susceptible populations and on DNA methylation.
captured_features: pregnancy outcomes (miscarriage, stillbirth, low birth weight, preterm birth, small for gestational age)
population: Pregnant women and children in Japan (Sapporo and Hokkaido cohorts)
exposure_domain: Environmental chemicals (dioxins, PCBs, organochlorine pesticides, PFAS, phthalates, BPA, methylmercury, tobacco smoking)
limitations: Ongoing study; continuous follow-up necessary to elucidate combined effects of chemical exposure
